title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: plan=Make `tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh` automatable by adding deterministic env-driven inputs (e.g., `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_CLI_DOC`, `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_SKILL_ROOT`, `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_NOTE_MANUSCRIPT_PATH`, `PHILOSOPHY_WORLDVIEW_LIVE_SMOKE_MODE=off`) plus a one-shot fallback when dotfiles are absent, and add a tiny normalization block so the same script can run in clean CI and user machines. integration=Integrate it through `scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh` as an explicit readiness subcheck (run static mode in `fugue-orchestration-gate.yml`, with optional local/manual invocation of `required` mode + injected temp HOME/dotfile staging when needed for lived smoke), because that gate is already the canonical kernel automation entry. codex_fix=Install the matching optional package into the exact runtime prefix (`/Users/masayuki/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0`) with `npm install -g @openai/codex-darwin-arm64@npm:@openai/codex@0.115.0-darwin-arm64` (or equivalent version-pin restore), which is the narrowest fix; avoid full global reinstall unless that package install still leaves `codex --version` failing.
